metadata.notes: agent_supports: yes (Task tool spawns a synchronous explore subagent); daemon_capability: bug (child written under <parent>/agents/<child>/ with parent_session_id in meta.json, but the vibe adapter never surfaces ParentSessionID so the link is dropped); driver_capability: gap:wait_turn

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: bug
driver_capability: gap:wait_turn
→ route: **driver-gap** (yes + bug + gap:* → driver-gap: record ports the wait_turn seam, then drives; the recording is known_failing on the parent↔child link because daemon=bug). Same route shape as the vibe 1-8/2-3/2-15 bug cells.

## The scenario's user-observable signal (3.1)

foreground-subagent is the SYNCHRONOUS variant of subagent dispatch: the parent spawns one child, the child appears with ParentSessionID = parent.session_id, SubagentCount(parent)=1 while the child is alive, the parent STAYS working until the child ends, then the parent goes working→ready. The distinguishing observables are the child session, its ParentSessionID linkage, the parent_linked event, and the parent staying working across the child's lifetime.

## agent_supports = yes

mistral-vibe 2.19.0 ships a first-class, SYNCHRONOUS subagent primitive — exactly what 3.1 asserts (this is the counterpart to 3-2_background-subagent, which is agent=no precisely because vibe has NO fire-and-forget/detached variant; the ONLY difference between the two scenarios is whether the parent finishes before the child drains, and vibe always awaits the child):

- `core/tools/builtins/task.py` `Task.run()` builds `subagent_loop = AgentLoop(config=..., agent_name=args.agent, is_subagent=True, ...)`, sets `subagent_loop.parent_session_id = ctx.session_id`, then drives it with `async with aclosing(subagent_loop.act(task_text)) as events: async for event in events: ...` and only AFTER the child loop drains does it `yield TaskResult(...)`. The parent's Task tool-call blocks on the child — a synchronous, in-process foreground subagent.
- The default subagent is `explore` (`TaskArgs.agent` defaults to `"explore"`; `core/agents/models.py` EXPLORE is `agent_type=AgentType.SUBAGENT`). The Task tool's `allowlist=[BuiltinAgentName.EXPLORE]` resolves to `ToolPermission.ALWAYS`, so a `task(agent=explore)` call auto-executes with NO permission prompt. The tool refuses any non-SUBAGENT agent ("security constraint to prevent recursive spawning").
- The `task` tool is advertised to the model in the system prompt (`core/system_prompt.py`: "The following subagents can be spawned via the Task tool"). Source scan: subagent (7 files), spawn (7), delegate (2), parent_session_id (13).

## daemon_capability = bug (the link is dropped, but the trace exists)

The child subagent DOES leave a local trace under the watched root, and the parent link IS on disk — the vibe adapter simply never surfaces it:

1. **Child writes a real session dir UNDER the watched FilesUnderRoot root.** `task.py` sets the child's `SessionLoggingConfig(save_dir=str(ctx.session_dir / "agents"), session_prefix=args.agent)`. `core/session/session_logger.py` composes the child dir as `save_dir/<prefix>_<timestamp>_<shortid>` and persists BOTH `messages.jsonl` (MESSAGES_FILENAME) and `meta.json` (METADATA_FILENAME) there. For a top-level parent whose session_dir is `~/.vibe/logs/session/<parent-id>/`, the child lands at `~/.vibe/logs/session/<parent-id>/agents/<explore_...>/messages.jsonl` — inside the adapter's `Source = FilesUnderRoot{~/.vibe/logs/session}`. `sessionIDFromPath` (adapter.go) accepts any-depth `messages.jsonl` and mints a session keyed on the child directory name, so the daemon WOULD observe the child as its own session (FilesUnderRoot tails arbitrarily-nested transcripts, as codex's sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/ layout proves).
2. **The parent link is written to the child's meta.json.** `SessionLogger` sets `session_metadata.parent_session_id = parent_session_id` (from `subagent_loop.parent_session_id = ctx.session_id`). Every live `~/.vibe/logs/session/*/meta.json` already carries a top-level `parent_session_id` key (null in all 6 captured sessions — none used a subagent). So a real child's meta.json would carry parent_session_id = the parent's session_id.
3. **The adapter DROPS the link.** `core/adapters/inbound/agents/vibe/parser.go` sets NO `ParentSessionID` on any ParsedEvent (it emits only user_message / assistant_message / tool_result / turn_done). `core/adapters/inbound/agents/vibe/sidecar.go` DOES read the child's meta.json — but decodes only `environment.working_directory`, `config.active_model`, and `stats.context_tokens`; it ignores `parent_session_id` entirely. And `deriveParentSession` (`core/application/services/helpers.go`) recognizes only the Claude-Code `.../<parent>/subagents/<agent>.jsonl` path, the Gemini registered-parent path, and the Pi `{"parentSession":...}` transcript header — a vibe child's `.../agents/<child>/messages.jsonl` path matches NONE, and its first messages.jsonl line is a plain message with no parentSession field.

Consequence: a vibe parent + spawned child surface as TWO UNRELATED top-level sessions. ParentSessionID stays empty, SubagentCount(parent)=0, no parent_linked event fires. This is `bug`, NOT `incapable`: the linking data (parent_session_id) is present in a sidecar the adapter ALREADY opens on every event — a feasible parser/discovery fix (sidecar.go decodes parent_session_id → parser sets ev.ParentSessionID, OR a vibe `.../agents/<child>/` branch in deriveParentSession) would bind child→parent with no new transport. The bug precedent on this column is 2-3/2-15/1-8 (a trace on disk the parser under-decodes).

## driver_capability = gap:wait_turn

The recipe (one Task-tool prompt, then block for the parent turn, then settle) needs send/wait_turn/sleep. The freshly-scaffolded `replaydata/agents/mistral-vibe/driver-interactive.sh` declares `DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep"` and stubs `wait_turn()` as a no-op — so wait_turn is grammatically present but NOT elicited (recipe_semantic_gaps → not-elicited:wait_turn). This is the standard vibe driver gap: nearly every recorded-shape vibe cell carries driver=gap:wait_turn; record ports the wait_turn seam from a reference driver before driving. send + sleep are elicited; no other primitive is needed.

## What would unblock (the bug fix)

Surface the parent link the child already writes: either (a) `sidecar.go` decodes `parent_session_id` and `parser.go` sets `ev.ParentSessionID` from it, or (b) add a vibe branch to `deriveParentSession` keyed on the `.../agents/<child>/messages.jsonl` path. Once child→parent binds, this cell flips from known_failing to a clean record (agent=yes, driver gap already ported).
caveats: No captured evidence of a real vibe subagent run: none of the 6 live ~/.vibe/logs/session sessions used the Task tool, so no `agents/` subtree exists on disk. The exact child layout (`<parent-session-dir>/agents/<prefix>_<ts>_<shortid>/{messages.jsonl,meta.json}` with parent_session_id populated) is inferred from task.py + session_logger.py source, not observed — confidence held at 0.8. A single live spawn settles the precise on-disk shape and confirms the watcher mints the child session at that nesting depth.

daemon=bug is PROVISIONAL on emission: it assumes the FilesUnderRoot watcher recurses into the nested agents/<child>/ dir and mints the child as a session (true for codex's deep layout, expected here). If the watcher for some reason did NOT observe the nested child, the same observables would be missing for a transport reason rather than a dropped link — but the fix owner (adapter) is unchanged. record settles which.

cost is not asserted: vibe's meta.json stats carries session_cost, but sidecar.go never reads it and mistral-vibe is absent from core/pkg/capacity, so the daemon emits no cost. observations assert model + tokens + agent only (matching the 2-1/2-3 vibe siblings).

sources.note: Task.run() spawns AgentLoop(is_subagent=True), sets subagent_loop.parent_session_id = ctx.session_id, and awaits the child (async for event in events) before yielding TaskResult — SYNCHRONOUS foreground subagent. Child SessionLoggingConfig save_dir = ctx.session_dir/'agents', session_prefix = args.agent (default 'explore', allowlist ALWAYS = no prompt)

child session_dir = save_dir/<prefix>_<timestamp>_<shortid>; persists messages.jsonl (MESSAGES_FILENAME) + meta.json (METADATA_FILENAME); begin_new_session sets session_metadata.parent_session_id = parent_session_id

EXPLORE = AgentProfile(agent_type=AgentType.SUBAGENT); Task tool refuses non-SUBAGENT agents ('security constraint to prevent recursive spawning'). 3-2_background-subagent is agent=no because vibe has ONLY this synchronous variant
sets NO ParentSessionID on any ParsedEvent — emits only user_message/assistant_message/tool_result/turn_done

reads the child's meta.json but decodes ONLY environment.working_directory, config.active_model, stats.context_tokens — ignores the top-level parent_session_id field the child writes (the droppable link; a feasible parser fix)

deriveParentSession recognizes ONLY claude .../<parent>/subagents/<agent>.jsonl, gemini registered-parent, and pi {parentSession:...} header — a vibe child's .../agents/<child>/messages.jsonl path + plain-message first line match none
Source = FilesUnderRoot{~/.vibe/logs/session}; sessionIDFromPath accepts any-depth messages.jsonl and keys the session on the parent directory name — mints the nested child as its own session
live meta.json carries a top-level parent_session_id key (null across all 6 captured sessions — none used a subagent) and config.active_model = mistral-medium-3.5
vibe 2.19.0 source scan: subagent (7 files incl task.py/manager.py/system_prompt.py), spawn (7), delegate (2), parent_session_id (13 incl session_logger.py/types.py)

preconditions: vibe CLI 2.19.0 on PATH and authenticated; the Task tool is exposed by default and agent=explore auto-executes (allowlist ALWAYS)
irrlichd is recording (dev daemon with --record, isolated IRRLICHT_HOME)
DRIVER GAP: driver-interactive.sh stubs wait_turn (DRIVE_ELICITS='send slash sleep'); record ports the wait_turn seam before driving

KNOWN FAILING: daemon=bug — the vibe adapter drops the child↔parent link (parent_session_id in the child meta.json is never surfaced as ParentSessionID), so parent_linked / SubagentCount / child-with-ParentSessionID will not be observed until the adapter fix lands
setup: Driver generates a fresh cwd under <staging>/cwd/
A README.md with a single H1 heading is seeded into the cwd so the subagent has a deterministic file to read

script.text: Use the Task tool to launch a subagent (agent: explore) that reads the README.md in the current directory and reports its first heading. Wait for the subagent to finish, then reply 'done'.

verify: the parent session enters ready, then ready→working on the user prompt
(known-failing until adapter fix) a child session materializes under <parent>/agents/<child>/messages.jsonl and is minted as a session
(known-failing until adapter fix) events.jsonl contains >= 1 parent_linked binding the child to the parent session_id (SubagentCount(parent)=1 while alive)
(known-failing until adapter fix) the parent stays working until the child ends, then a single working→ready
